intro: Huberia argentea is a species of flowering plant in the family Melastomataceae, commonly known for its medicinal properties. The leaves and roots of H. argentea have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. The bioactive constituents of H. argentea include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, such as quercetin and isorhapontigenin, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, H. argentea is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation.
